title: Phrasal Verbs — Travel
description: Phrasal verbs are everywhere in travel English — check in, take off, get around, look out.
summary_pt_br: Phrasal verbs de viagem: check in, check out, take off, get around, look out for, pick up, set off.

scenes.subtitle_en: Phrasal verbs are everywhere in travel English — check in, take off, get around, look out.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Phrasal verbs de viagem: check in, check out, take off, get around, look out for, pick up, set off.

scenes.text_en: Phrasal verbs are everywhere in travel English — check in, take off, get around, look out. Today's focus: phrasal verbs — travel. We check in at the hotel at three o'clock.
scenes.text_pt: Phrasal verbs de viagem: check in, check out, take off, get around, look out for, pick up, set off. Foco de hoje: phrasal verbs — travel. Fazemos o check-in no hotel às três.

scenes.title: Phrasal Verbs — Travel
scenes.body_en: Common travel phrasal verbs: CHECK IN (hotel/airport), CHECK OUT (leave hotel), TAKE OFF (airplane departs), GET AROUND (move in a city), LOOK OUT FOR (be careful of), PICK UP (collect someone), SET OFF (start a journey).
scenes.body_pt: Phrasal verbs de viagem: check in, check out, take off, get around, look out for, pick up, set off.

examples.en: We check in at the hotel at three o'clock.
examples.pt: Fazemos o check-in no hotel às três.

examples.en: The plane takes off in twenty minutes.
examples.pt: O avião decola em vinte minutos.

examples.en: In Amsterdam, we got around by bicycle.
examples.pt: Em Amsterdã, a gente se virava de bicicleta.

exercise.question: Complete: "We check in at the hotel at three ___."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "We check in at the hotel at three o'clock."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Fazemos o check-in no hotel às três.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
The plane takes off in twenty minutes.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "The plane takes off in twenty minutes."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O avião decola em vinte minutos.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Phrasal Verbs — Travel — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre phrasal verbs — travel. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: So, you're finally traveling to Portugal. When do you set off?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I set off on Thursday at six in the morning.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What time does your flight take off?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It takes off at eight fifteen from the main terminal.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Is someone picking you up in Lisbon?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, my cousin is picking me up at the airport.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How are you planning to get around the city?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I will get around by metro and walking.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What time will you check in at the hotel?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I check in at three in the afternoon.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Perfect. Have a wonderful trip and look out for pickpockets.

scenes.title: Zara's first day in Lisbon
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write what Zara does from the airport to the hotel. Use at least three travel phrasal verbs.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Zara's plane took off from London just after dawn and landed in Lisbon three hours later.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: I picked up my luggage quickly and looked out for my cousin at the exit.

paragraphs.speaker: Cousin
paragraphs.en: Welcome! I'll drive you to the hotel so you can check in and rest.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Can we set off early tomorrow? I want to get around the old quarter on foot.

paragraphs.speaker: Cousin
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. We'll check out of the hotel after breakfast and start exploring.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Travel, as Zara liked to say, is a short course in every phrasal verb you will ever need.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Phrasal Verbs — Travel". Phrasal verbs are everywhere in travel English — check in, take off, get around, look out. We check in at the hotel at three o'clock. The plane takes off in twenty minutes. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Phrasal Verbs — Travel". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Phrasal Verbs — Travel".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
